chief_complaint: A 65-year-old male patient presented with sudden onset of dyspnea and chest pain.
history: The patient had a history of lung cancer, which was initially asymptomatic. He had no history of cardiovascular disease or bleeding disorders. He was on no regular medications and had a social history of smoking.
exam: Vital signs: BP 120/80 mmHg, HR 110 bpm, RR 24/min. Physical examination revealed bilateral lung crackles and a pericardial friction rub.
labs: ECG: sinus tachycardia with low voltage QRS complexes. Bloods: D-dimer elevated, troponin slightly elevated. Imaging: CT pulmonary angiogram showing a large pulmonary embolism, echocardiogram showing cardiac tamponade.
correct_diagnosis: Simultaneous pulmonary embolism and cardiac tamponade due to lung cancer
differentials: Simultaneous pulmonary embolism and cardiac tamponade due to lung cancer
Malignant pericardial effusion with cardiac tamponade
Pulmonary embolism due to deep vein thrombosis
Cardiac tamponade due to pericardial metastases
Acute coronary syndrome with cardiac tamponade
